messages.content:
You are ClawdBot, a highly capable AI assistant on Discord powered by Gemini.
You are helpful, concise, and proactive. You have access to various tools including:
- Google Calendar (list, create, update, delete events)
- Gmail (search and read emails, read-only)
- File management (read, write, list files in a sandboxed workspace)
- Web search and page reading (search the web, fetch and read URLs)
- Long-term memory (store and recall facts about users)
- Scheduled tasks (create recurring tasks that run on a cron schedule)
- Self-expansion (you can create new tools/plugins to gain new capabilities)

Guidelines:
- Use tools proactively when the user's request implies them.
- For calendar modifications, briefly confirm what you're about to do.
- When asked to do something you can't do yet, offer to create a new tool using create_tool.
- Keep responses concise but informative.
- When showing email content, respect privacy and summarize unless asked for full text.
- Format responses nicely using Discord markdown (bold, code blocks, etc).
- Current date context will be provided in messages.
- When the user asks to schedule a recurring task, use schedule_task with a cron expression.
- Proactively use memory_store to remember important user details for future conversations.

## Known facts about this user (from long-term memory):
- [project] Daily TikTok script generation job: read 'tiktokprompt.md' (root workspace file), pick random psychological topic, fill inputs, generate Hungarian TikTok voiceover scripts (45–55s, per provided blueprint), post daily at 06:00 in the channel that created the task. Retry once on failure, log detailed runtime and metadata.
- [project] Per-minute Kecskemét weather updates scheduled: fetch Open-Meteo current_weather for lat=46.896 lon=19.689 and post concise summary to the requesting channel every minute. Logs saved to logs/weather_kecskemet.log. Retry once on failure; fallback to wttr.in JSON.

[Current date/time: 2026-02-28T12:24:42.217Z]

Every minute, fetch current weather for Kecskemét (latitude 46.896, longitude 19.689) and post a concise weather summary to the channel that created the task. Steps: 1) Request current weather from Open-Meteo API: https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=46.896&longitude=19.689&current_weather=true&timezone=Europe%2FBudapest . 2) Parse current_weather fields (temperature, windspeed, winddirection, weathercode, time). 3) Map weathercode to a short human-readable description (use Open-Meteo weathercode mapping). 4) Compose a single short message in Hungarian or English (prefer Hungarian if channel language is Hungarian): e.g., "Kecskemét most: 12.3°C, Derült/Enyhén felhős (description), szél 3.4 m/s, idő: 2026-02-28 13:45 CET." Keep message to one or two lines. Include the data timestamp and source URL. 5) Post the message to the channel (only the message; no extra commentary). 6) Logging & error handling: - Log start/end timestamps, API URL, HTTP status, full API response (or error), parsed fields, and posted message to the bot runtime logs (workspace logs). - If the fetch or parsing fails, retry once silently. - If retry fails, post a short one-line error message to the channel with the date/time and a one-line failure summary (do not include stack traces). - Always save detailed logs in workspace logs (e.g., logs/weather_kecskemet.log) including full error details and metadata. 7) Rate & etiquette: run every minute as requested. Ensure messages are brief to avoid flooding large amounts of text. 8) Do not store or expose any sensitive credentials. 9) If the Open-Meteo API changes or is unavailable, try wttr.in JSON as fallback: https://wttr.in/Kecskemét?format=j1 .

function.name: schedule_task
function.arguments: {"cron_expression":"*/1 * * * *","task_description":"Every minute, in the channel that created this task, perform the following and post only the single-line or two-line weather summary message (Hungarian if the channel language is Hungarian; otherwise English). Steps: 1) Fetch current weather from Open-Meteo API URL: https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=46.896&longitude=19.689&current_weather=true&timezone=Europe%2FBudapest . 2) If the fetch fails or response is invalid, retry once silently; if retry also fails, post one short error line to the channel (include date/time and one-line failure summary) and write full error details to logs/weather_kecskemet.log. 3) If fetch succeeds, parse JSON.current_weather and extract these fields: temperature (°C), windspeed (m/s), winddirection (degrees), weathercode (integer), time (ISO, in Europe/Budapest timezone). 4) Map weathercode to a short human-readable description using Open-Meteo mapping. Use Hungarian descriptions when posting in Hungarian. Mapping (code: EN -> HU): 0: Clear sky -> Derült; 1: Mainly clear -> Főként derült; 2: Partly cloudy -> Részben felhős; 3: Overcast -> Borult; 45: Fog -> Köd; 48: Depositing rime fog -> Rime-köd; 51: Light drizzle -> Enyhe szitálás; 53: Moderate drizzle -> Mérsékelt szitálás; 55: Dense drizzle -> Erős szitálás; 56: Light freezing drizzle -> Enyhe fagyos szitálás; 57: Dense freezing drizzle -> Erős fagyos szitálás; 61: Slight rain -> Enyhe eső; 63: Moderate rain -> Mérsékelt eső; 65: Heavy rain -> Erős eső; 66: Light freezing rain -> Enyhe ónos eső; 67: Heavy freezing rain -> Erős ónos eső; 71: Slight snow fall -> Enyhe havazás; 73: Moderate snow fall -> Mérsékelt havazás; 75: Heavy snow fall -> Erős havazás; 77: Snow grains -> Hópelyhek; 80: Slight rain showers -> Enyhe zápor; 81: Moderate rain showers -> Mérsékelt zápor; 82: Violent rain showers -> Erős zápor; 85: Slight snow showers -> Enyhe hózápor; 86: Heavy snow showers -> Erős hózápor; 95: Thunderstorm -> Zivatar; 96: Thunderstorm with slight hail -> Zivatar, enyhe jégverés; 99: Thunderstorm with heavy hail -> Zivatar, heves jégverés. 5) Compose a concise message (one or two lines) in Hungarian (or English fallback) using this format example in Hungarian: \"Kecskemét most: 12.3°C, Derült, szél 3.4 m/s (winddir 180°), idő: 2026-02-28 13:45 CET. For English use: \"Kecskemét now: 12.3°C, Clear sky, wind 3.4 m/s (180°), time: 2026-02-28 13:45 CET.\" Include the data timestamp (local Europe/Budapest), and include the source URL at the end of the message. 6) If Open-Meteo API is unavailable or response format changed, fetch fallback JSON from wttr.in: https://wttr.in/Kecskemét?format=j1 and parse the nearest current conditions to obtain temperature (°C), wind speed (m/s or km/h convert to m/s), weather description, and local time; then compose message similarly and post. 7) Logging: for every run append a detailed log entry to workspace file logs/weather_kecskemet.log containing start timestamp, end timestamp, API URL used, HTTP status, full API response (or full error details), parsed fields, posted message text, and retry attempts. If an error occurs, include full error stack/details in the log but only post a one-line failure message to the channel. 8) Rate & etiquette: keep posted messages short (one or two lines). 9) Do not store or expose any sensitive credentials. 10) Retry logic: if first fetch or parsing fails, retry once; if retry fails, log details and post one-line error. 11) Always include the source URL in the posted message. 12) File paths: append logs to logs/weather_kecskemet.log. 13) If successful, post only the composed weather message to the channel (no extra commentary)."}

It will run on cron \"*/1 * * * *\" in this channel."}

messages.content: {"tasks":[{"id":7,"schedule":"0 8-20 * * *","description":"On creation: 1) Validate cron expression exactly '0 8-20 * * *'. 2) Validate that file 'tiktokprompt.md' exists at workspace root and is parseable (if missing or not parseable, fail creation and post a short error to the channel that created the task; do not register the task). 3) Validate output format requirement: generated script must be returned and posted as a single codeblock only (no extra text). 4) Perform a dry-run/test run immediately (DO NOT post to channel); generate one Hungarian TikTok voiceover script using the instructions in tiktokprompt.md, pick a random psychological topic, fill inputs, and log the dry-run result (include timestamp, word count, estimated duration, success/failure, and full generated output). On each scheduled run (cron: 0 8-20 * * *; post to the channel that created this task): - Read 'tiktokprompt.md' from workspace root (filepath exactly 'tiktokprompt.md'). - Pick a random psychological topic (one named concept). - Fill the inputs in the prompt and generate the TikTok script following the blueprint and content rules in tiktokprompt.md (maintain Hungarian voiceover length ~45–55s unless tiktokprompt.md specifies otherwise). - Format the script inside one codeblock and post only that codeblock to the channel (nothing before or after). - Log runtime (start/end timestamps), success/failure, picked topic, word count, estimated duration, retries performed, and any relevant metadata. Steps: 1) Fetch current weather from Open-Meteo API URL: https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=46.896&longitude=19.689&current_weather=true&timezone=Europe%2FBudapest . 2) If the fetch fails or response is invalid, retry once silently; if retry also fails, post one short error line to the channel (include date/time and one-line failure summary) and write full error details to logs/weather_kecskemet.log. 3) If fetch succeeds, parse JSON.current_weather and extract these fields: temperature (°C), windspeed (m/s), winddirection (degrees), weathercode (integer), time (ISO, in Europe/Budapest timezone). 4) Map weathercode to a short human-readable description using Open-Meteo mapping. Use Hungarian descriptions when posting in Hungarian. Mapping (code: EN -> HU): 0: Clear sky -> Derült; 1: Mainly clear -> Főként derült; 2: Partly cloudy -> Részben felhős; 3: Overcast -> Borult; 45: Fog -> Köd; 48: Depositing rime fog -> Rime-köd; 51: Light drizzle -> Enyhe szitálás; 53: Moderate drizzle -> Mérsékelt szitálás; 55: Dense drizzle -> Erős szitálás; 56: Light freezing drizzle -> Enyhe fagyos szitálás; 57: Dense freezing drizzle -> Erős fagyos szitálás; 61: Slight rain -> Enyhe eső; 63: Moderate rain -> Mérsékelt eső; 65: Heavy rain -> Erős eső; 66: Light freezing rain -> Enyhe ónos eső; 67: Heavy freezing rain -> Erős ónos eső; 71: Slight snow fall -> Enyhe havazás; 73: Moderate snow fall -> Mérsékelt havazás; 75: Heavy snow fall -> Erős havazás; 77: Snow grains -> Hópelyhek; 80: Slight rain showers -> Enyhe zápor; 81: Moderate rain showers -> Mérsékelt zápor; 82: Violent rain showers -> Erős zápor; 85: Slight snow showers -> Enyhe hózápor; 86: Heavy snow showers -> Erős hózápor; 95: Thunderstorm -> Zivatar; 96: Thunderstorm with slight hail -> Zivatar, enyhe jégverés; 99: Thunderstorm with heavy hail -> Zivatar, heves jégverés. 5) Compose a concise message (one or two lines) in Hungarian (or English fallback) using this format example in Hungarian: "Kecskemét most: 12.3°C, Derült, szél 3.4 m/s (winddir 180°), idő: 2026-02-28 13:45 CET. For English use: "Kecskemét now: 12.3°C, Clear sky, wind 3.4 m/s (180°), time: 2026-02-28 13:45 CET." Include the data timestamp (local Europe/Budapest), and include the source URL at the end of the message. 6) If Open-Meteo API is unavailable or response format changed, fetch fallback JSON from wttr.in: https://wttr.in/Kecskemét?format=j1 and parse the nearest current conditions to obtain temperature (°C), wind speed (m/s or km/h convert to m/s), weather description, and local time; then compose message similarly and post. 7) Logging: for every run append a detailed log entry to workspace file logs/weather_kecskemet.log containing start timestamp, end timestamp, API URL used, HTTP status, full API response (or full error details), parsed fields, posted message text, and retry attempts. If an error occurs, include full error stack/details in the log but only post a one-line failure message to the channel. 8) Rate & etiquette: keep posted messages short (one or two lines). 9) Do not store or expose any sensitive credentials. 10) Retry logic: if first fetch or parsing fails, retry once; if retry fails, log details and post one-line error. 11) Always include the source URL in the posted message. 12) File paths: append logs to logs/weather_kecskemet.log. 13) If successful, post only the composed weather message to the channel (no extra commentary).
